content.problemStatement:
The beauty value of an array is defined as the difference between the largest and second largest elements of the array. Note that the largest and second largest elements can have the same value in case of duplicates.

For example, beauty value of [2,5,3,1]=5−3=2[2, 5, 3, 1] = 5 - 3 = 2[2,5,3,1]=5−3=2 and beauty value of [7,6,7]=7−7=0[7, 6, 7] = 7 - 7 = 0[7,6,7]=7−7=0

You are given an array AAA of length NNN. Your task is to find the total number of **distinct** beauty values among all subarrays of AAA having length greater than 111.

Note that, a subarray is obtained by deleting some (possibly zero) elements from the beginning and some (possibly zero) elements from the end of the array.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN — the size of the array.
The second line contains NNN space-separated numbers - A1,A2,…,ANA_{1}, A_{2},\ldots ,A_{N}A1​,A2​,…,AN​, the elements of the array.




Output Format
For each test case, output a single line, the total number of **distinct** beauty among all subarrays of AAA having length greater than 111.
constraints: Sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The only subarray is [1,1][1,1][1,1] whose beauty is 000. Thus, there is only 111 distinct value of beauty.
Test case 222: The subarrays are [4,2],[2,1],[4,2], [2,1],[4,2],[2,1], and [4,2,1][4,2,1][4,2,1] having beauty 2,1,2, 1,2,1, and 222 respectively. There are 222 distinct values of beauty.
Test case 333: The unique values of beauty are 7,1,6,7,1,6,7,1,6, and 555.
Test case 444: The unique values of beauty are 3,5,2,3,5,2,3,5,2, and 111.
